title: Pure Velvety Spanish Casabe Cocido Madrileno
description: A low sodium, velvety Spanish dish featuring casabe, cooked in a clay pot, originating from the Andalusian region, this recipe is a variation of the traditional Cocido Madrileno
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large clay pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth, casabe, and chickpeas, and stir to combine.
Cover the clay pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until the casabe is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the clay pot from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the Pure Velvety Spanish Casabe Cocido Madrileno hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
